title: Add more visual effects and graphical enhancements to the 3D fighting game

requirements.text: Add a muzzle flash effect in Scene3D.tsx that fires when UG shoots — render a brief burst of bright emissive geometry (e.g., a small flattened sphere or cross of planes) at the gun muzzle position, visible for 2–4 frames then disappearing.
acceptanceCriteria: A visible flash appears at UG's gun muzzle position each time a projectile is fired.
The flash is visually bright/emissive and disappears within a few frames.
No persistent visual artifact is left after the flash fades.

requirements.text: Add an explosion effect in Scene3D.tsx or ParticleSystem3D.tsx that triggers when a projectile hits Sukuna — render an expanding burst of emissive sphere/plane meshes or instanced particles with fiery colors (orange, red, gold) that scale up and fade out over ~0.4 seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: An explosion visual effect appears at the point of projectile impact on Sukuna.
The explosion uses fiery/cursed-energy colors consistent with the existing theme.
The effect scales up and fades out within approximately 0.4 seconds.
No persistent geometry remains after the explosion animation completes.

requirements.text: Add a persistent cursed-energy aura effect around Sukuna in Scene3D.tsx — render a slowly pulsing semi-transparent emissive mesh (e.g., an enlarged sphere or distorted shell) in deep crimson/dark magenta around Sukuna's body that scales slightly in and out over time to convey supernatural energy.
acceptanceCriteria: Sukuna has a visible pulsing aura effect rendered around their mesh at all times during battle.
The aura uses deep crimson or dark magenta emissive coloring.
The aura subtly scales or pulses in a looping animation rather than remaining static.
The effect does not obscure Sukuna's character mesh.

requirements.text: Add a ground impact shockwave effect in Scene3D.tsx triggered when either character lands from a jump — render a flat disc mesh that rapidly expands outward along the ground plane and fades in opacity over ~0.3 seconds, using the character's team color (blue for UG, red for Sukuna).
acceptanceCriteria: A shockwave disc appears on the ground at the character's landing position when they land from a jump.
The disc expands radially and fades out over approximately 0.3 seconds.
UG's shockwave uses blue/cool coloring; Sukuna's uses red/warm coloring.
No shockwave is shown when the character is simply walking (only on jump landing).

requirements.text: Add animated energy projectile trails in Scene3D.tsx — as each gun projectile travels, emit a stream of small emissive particles (or use a tapered instanced mesh ribbon) behind it in electric gold/cyan color that fade quickly, reinforcing the sense of speed and energy.
acceptanceCriteria: Traveling projectiles have a visible trailing particle or ribbon effect behind them.
Trail uses electric gold or cyan emissive coloring.
Trail particles fade quickly so they don't linger beyond 0.2 seconds after the projectile has passed.
The trail does not appear for melee attacks, only for gun projectiles.

requirements.text: Add a dynamic screen-edge vignette or flash overlay in the React DOM layer (App.tsx or FightingGame.tsx) that briefly flashes red at the edges of the viewport when UG takes damage, giving tactile feedback for being hit.
acceptanceCriteria: A red semi-transparent vignette/border flash is visible around the screen edges when UG takes a hit.
The flash fades out within approximately 0.5 seconds.
The effect does not block game controls or obscure health bars.
The effect triggers only when UG loses health, not when Sukuna takes damage.

constraints: All new effects must be consistent with the existing bold, dramatic, cursed-energy visual theme.
New particle/mesh effects must not significantly degrade rendering performance — reuse instanced meshes where possible.
Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or any files under frontend/src/components/ui.
Game logic (physics, AI, combat, health) must remain unchanged.
nonGoals: No new gameplay mechanics or weapons.
No changes to backend Motoko code.
No changes to calendar or sanctuary features.
No changes to on-screen control layout or keyboard bindings.
